 33→class CsvImporter : PageFragment() {
 34→    override fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): PageWebViewClient {
 35→        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
 36→        val backCallback =
 37→            object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
 38→                override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
 39→                    CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
 40→                    // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
 41→                    remove()
 42→                }
 43→            }
 44→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 45→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
 46→        return CsvImporterWebViewClient(backCallback)
 47→    }
 48→
 49→    override fun onViewCreated(
 50→        view: View,
 51→        savedInstanceState: Bundle?,
 52→    ) {
 53→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 54→        requireActivity().setTransparentStatusBar()
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    inner class CsvImporterWebViewClient(
 58→        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback,
 59→    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
 60→        /**
 61→         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
 62→         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
 63→         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
 64→         */
 65→        private var isDone = false
 66→
 67→        override fun onPageFinished(
 68→            view: WebView?,
 69→            url: String?,
 70→        ) {
 71→            view!!.evaluateJavascript(hideShowButtonCss) {
 72→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
 73→            }
 74→        }
 75→
 76→        override fun onLoadResource(
 77→            view: WebView?,
 78→            url: String?,
 79→        ) {
 80→            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
 81→            backCallback.isEnabled =
 82→                when {
 83→                    url == null -> false
 84→                    url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
 85→                    url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
 86→                        isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
 87→                        false
 88→                    }
 89→                    else -> false
 90→                }
 91→        }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    companion object {
 95→        /**
 96→         * @param filePath path of the csv file that will be imported, which should be accessible by AnkiDroid
 97→         * @return an intent to open the [CsvImporter] page on [SingleFragmentActivity]
 98→         */
 99→        fun getIntent(
100→            context: Context,
101→            filePath: String,
102→        ): Intent {
103→            val title = context.getString(R.string.menu_import)
104→            return getIntent(context, "import-csv$filePath", title, CsvImporter::class)
105→        }

 26→/**
 27→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 28→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 29→ * and this routine will return false.
 30→ *
 31→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 32→ *
 33→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 34→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 35→ * to check for success.
 36→ *
 37→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 38→ *
 39→ */
 40→fun Collection.createBackup(
 41→    backupFolder: String,
 42→    force: Boolean,
 43→    waitForCompletion: Boolean,
 44→): Boolean =
 45→    backend.createBackup(
 46→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 47→        force = force,
 48→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion,
 49→    )
 50→
 51→/**
 52→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 53→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 54→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 55→ */
 56→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
